description: HTTP/3 (QUIC) attacks — 0-RTT replay, stream-reset poisoning, connection-migration auth bypass, downgrade to HTTP/2 or HTTP/1.1. Reference-only for now: requires HTTP/3 client tooling. Note: this file is in REFERENCE_ONLY in scan.py.

zero_rtt_replay.description: QUIC 0-RTT (TLS 1.3 early data) — server accepts requests on first flight before handshake completes. If application processes state-changing requests in 0-RTT, replay is possible (RFC 9001 §9.2 forbids this but some deployments allow).

probes.description: Send identical state-changing POST twice via 0-RTT. If both succeed (e.g. coupon applied twice, balance debited twice), 0-RTT replay is allowed. Requires HTTP/3 client (e.g. quiche, ngtcp2, curl with --http3).

stream_reset_poisoning.description: QUIC STREAM frames followed by RESET_STREAM. Some HTTP/3 servers process partial requests before honoring reset. Variant of CVE-2023-44487 (HTTP/2 Rapid Reset) for QUIC.

probes.description: Open N streams, send headers + RESET_STREAM immediately. If server processes the request anyway (logs in DB, queues job), DoS or unauthenticated execution is possible.

connection_migration_auth.description: QUIC connection migration — connection ID survives client IP changes. Some servers bind session/auth to (5-tuple) and break on migration; others trust the ID and allow auth bypass when attacker captures a connection ID.

probes.description: Capture connection ID, send NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames from attacker IP, then send authenticated request. If server accepts under new path challenge, auth-bound-to-IP is broken.

alt_svc_downgrade.description: Alt-Svc header advertising HTTP/3 endpoint that differs in trust boundary from HTTP/2. Some clients pin to advertised endpoint without validating cert chain matches.

probes.description: Read Alt-Svc header. If endpoint is on different host/port without strict cert pinning, attacker on path can MITM the HTTP/3 connection.

craft_guidance.tooling: Use curl --http3 (curl 7.66+ with quiche/openssl-quic), ngtcp2 examples, or burp-http3 extension. Most Burp Suite versions DO NOT proxy HTTP/3; check via check_pro_features and fall back to direct QUIC client + manual replay.
craft_guidance.fingerprint: Detect HTTP/3 support via Alt-Svc: h3=":443" header on HTTP/2 response. Confirm with: curl -v --http3 https://target
craft_guidance.scope: 0-RTT replay only matters for state-changing endpoints. Test login, payment, vote, transfer, coupon-apply.
